measurements.claim: The seventeenth-wave package adds a bounded Lean typed finite-grid replay rung while keeping smooth periodic integration by parts NOT_REPLAYED and the parent Millennium problem UNVERIFIABLE.
evidence: the package describes one kernel-checked typed finite-grid replay rung
the package states smooth periodic integration by parts is not replayed
the package states the parent Navier-Stokes Millennium problem remains UNVERIFIABLE

measurements.claim: The seventeenth-wave theorem is stored in a separate Lean file and preserves the fourteenth-, fifteenth-, and sixteenth-wave source hashes.
evidence: lean-typed-finite-grid replay receipt records all prior source hashes as preserved

measurements.claim: TypedFiniteGridSummationByPartsPreflight.lean compiles with Lean exit code 0 and replays GridSample, CyclicGrid, lastSample, gradientOpen, divergenceInterior, cyclicGradientSum, cyclicDivergenceSum, open_grid_summation_by_parts, and typed_finite_grid_summation_by_parts_cancels.
The seventeenth-wave source refresh retains 8 arXiv metadata rows, 8 unique IDs, verifies both Gather stores as MATCH, and demotes the rows to SOURCE_LEAD_ONLY.
The website copy and official-copy scaffold distinguish TYPED_FINITE_GRID_SBP_MATCH from NOT_REPLAYED smooth theorem claims.
evidence: research-formal-replay-preflight.html states TYPED_FINITE_GRID_SBP_MATCH for the typed finite-grid theorem
research-formal-replay-preflight.html states continuous periodic integration by parts is NOT_REPLAYED

measurements.claim: The seventeenth-wave content queue includes explicit do-not-post boundaries for Navier-Stokes, smooth periodic integration by parts, source truth, exhaustive coverage, native BuildLang relation receipts, and accepted-publication claims.
evidence: Do Not Post forbids Project Telos solved Navier-Stokes
Do Not Post forbids Lean proved smooth periodic integration by parts
Do Not Post forbids arXiv metadata proves paper truth
Do Not Post forbids latest/exhaustive coverage
Do Not Post forbids native BuildLang relation-invariant support
Do Not Post forbids website copy is submitted, accepted, or peer-reviewed
